title: First Missing Positive
description: Given an unsorted integer array nums . Return the smallest positive integer that is not present in nums . You must implement an algorithm that runs in O(n) time and uses O(1) auxiliary space.   Example 1: Input: nums = [1,2,0] Output: 3 Explanation: The numbers in the range [1,2] are all in the array. Example 2: Input: nums = [3,4,-1,1] Output: 2 Explanation: 1 is in the array but 2 is missing. Example 3: Input: nums = [7,8,9,11,12] Output: 1 Explanation: The smallest positive integer 1 is missing.   Constraints: 1 <= nums.length <= 10 5 -2 31 <= nums[i] <= 2 31 - 1
hints: Think about how you would solve the problem in non-constant space.  Can you apply that logic to the existing space?
We don't care about duplicates or non-positive integers
